title: Olive Oil on Linen
timeEstimate: 20–40 minutes for washable unstructured linen; longer if oil has aged
stainChemistry: Olive oil is treated as an oil/fat stain. Detergent/surfactant pretreat is source-backed, but linen handling must be bounded to washable, unstructured, colorfast items. Home guidance excludes branded detergent claims and professional solvents/acids.
whyThisWorks: Blotting removes excess oil without spreading it. Generic liquid or heavy-duty laundry detergent supplies surfactants that help suspend oily soil so care-label laundering can carry it away. Air-drying before heat prevents residual oil from setting or darkening.
scienceNote: This card does not claim all linen blends behave the same. Rayon blends, structured/valuable linen, vintage pieces, unstable dyes, and dry-clean-only construction require professional review.
defaultAssumption: Use only for washable, unstructured linen or flat linen goods. Escalate dry-clean-only, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye linen.

spottingProtocol.technique: Care-label, construction, fiber, and colorfastness assessment
spottingProtocol.equipment: Clean white cloth, hidden seam/hem test
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Stop immediately on transfer or change
spottingProtocol.instruction: Confirm linen is safe for wet treatment and document any Home attempts. Test colorfastness on a hidden seam or hem before any spotting.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only intake. Follow care label; reject or escalate unstable dye, structured construction, or high-value textiles.

spottingProtocol.technique: Use the mildest source-supported professional path first
spottingProtocol.equipment: Product-labeled detergent or family-specific spotter; clean towels
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Use product-label dwell only; stop on dye movement or texture change
spottingProtocol.instruction: Treat olive oil on linen as a oil/fat stain and begin with controlled detergent/water action before any stronger chemistry. Keep chemistry bounded by SDS/label and fabric test results.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only. Use PPE/ventilation required by product SDS; do not expose Home users to pro solvents, acids, oxidizers, or concentrated spotters.

spottingProtocol.technique: Flush between products and inspect dry
spottingProtocol.equipment: Water/steam only if fabric and product label permit; drying rack
spottingProtocol.dwellTime: Until residues are flushed and fabric is dry enough to inspect
spottingProtocol.instruction: Flush or rinse out chemistry before switching products. Air-dry/inspect before considering any heat, oxidizer, solvent, or additional professional escalation.
spottingProtocol.safetyNote: Pro-only. Do not mix incompatible chemistry; use PPE/ventilation and document any last-resort escalation.

products: Product-labeled professional oil/detergent systems only, used per SDS/label
professionalProtocol.temperature: Cool or care-label-safe lukewarm; professional heat only if care label, construction, and test results permit
warnings: Pro-only oil/solvent path requires SDS/label controls, gloves/eye protection, ventilation, linen hem test, and flush/rinse controls.

homeSolutions.agent: Care Label and Linen Construction Check
homeSolutions.instruction: Confirm the linen is washable and unstructured. Stop for dry-clean-only, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye linen.
Blot with a clean white cloth or lift excess oil gently. Do not rub and do not spread the oil halo.
On colored linen, test a small amount of detergent and cool water on a hidden hem with a clean white cloth. Stop if color transfers or texture changes.
Apply a small amount of generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent to the oily area. Work it in gently with a fingertip or soft cloth; do not scrub.
Wash according to the care label using cool or care-label-safe lukewarm water. Avoid hot water unless the care label clearly permits it and the fabric is stable.
Air-dry flat or as the care label directs. Inspect before any dryer or iron. Repeat once if improving; escalate for spreading halo, ring, dye bleed, texture change, or no improvement.

steps: 1. Care Label and Linen Construction Check: Confirm the linen is washable and unstructured. Stop for dry-clean-only, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye linen.
2. Blot or Lift Excess Oil: Blot with a clean white cloth or lift excess oil gently. Do not rub and do not spread the oil halo.
3. Hidden Hem Test: On colored linen, test a small amount of detergent and cool water on a hidden hem with a clean white cloth. Stop if color transfers or texture changes.

4. Generic Liquid or Heavy-Duty Detergent: Apply a small amount of generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent to the oily area. Work it in gently with a fingertip or soft cloth; do not scrub.
5. Cool/Lukewarm Care-Label Wash: Wash according to the care label using cool or care-label-safe lukewarm water. Avoid hot water unless the care label clearly permits it and the fabric is stable.

6. Air-Dry and Inspect: Air-dry flat or as the care label directs. Inspect before any dryer or iron. Repeat once if improving; escalate for spreading halo, ring, dye bleed, texture change, or no improvement.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Escalate spreading oil halo, ring formation, dye bleed, texture change, structured/valuable/vintage/dry-clean-only linen, unstable dye, or no improvement after one or two detergent attempts.
warnings: No Home dry-side solvents, concentrated acids, strong alkali, or professional oil removers
No heat until oil is gone

neverDo: Never put the item in a dryer, use an iron, or apply heat until the stain is gone after air-drying inspection
Never rub or scrub aggressively; blot, lift, or gently work only enough to avoid driving soil deeper
Never mix cleaning agents, bleach, ammonia, acids, solvents, or professional spotters
Never use chlorine bleach unless the care label allows it and the fabric is white or confirmed colorfast
Never use professional-strength oxidizers, acids, dry-side solvents, rust/mineral removers, adhesive removers, or named pro products in the Home path
Never use chlorine bleach on colored linen
Never apply hot water, steam, hot dryer, or iron to linen with residual oil
Never use dry-side solvents, concentrated acids, strong alkali, or professional oil removers in the Home path
fiberSensitivities: Linen is cellulose/flax; colored linen needs controlled temperature and a hidden hem/colorfastness test
Structured, lined, embellished, vintage, high-value, dry-clean-only, or unstable-dye linen can distort, bleed, or lose finish under wet treatment and should route to Pro
Avoid abrasion and over-wetting structured linen
chemicalBoundaries: Home path is limited to water, generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent, and care-label laundering
Use any detergent, enzyme detergent, or pretreat only according to its product label and only after a hidden-area test on colored fabric
Oxidizers are not routine Home steps; any trace-color escalation must be care-label-safe, white/colorfast, hidden-test-passed, and never mixed with other cleaners
Professional chemistry is Pro-only and requires SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, colorfastness testing, and flush/rinse controls
Home oil path is generic liquid/heavy-duty laundry detergent only; oxidizers are not needed for the oil Home path
Professional solvent or stronger oil chemistry is Pro-only with SDS/label, PPE/ventilation, linen hem test, and flush/rinse controls
chemicalControls: Home path is limited to water, generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent, and care-label laundering
Use any detergent, enzyme detergent, or pretreat only according to its product label and only after a hidden-area test on colored fabric
Oxidizers are not routine Home steps; any trace-color escalation must be care-label-safe, white/colorfast, hidden-test-passed, and never mixed with other cleaners
Professional chemistry is Pro-only and requires SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, colorfastness testing, and flush/rinse controls
Home oil path is generic liquid/heavy-duty laundry detergent only; oxidizers are not needed for the oil Home path
Professional solvent or stronger oil chemistry is Pro-only with SDS/label, PPE/ventilation, linen hem test, and flush/rinse controls
homeAllowed: Cool/cold water or care-label-safe lukewarm water
Generic liquid laundry detergent or heavy-duty liquid laundry detergent
proOnly: Professional spotters, dry-side solvents, acids, oxidizers, reducers, concentrated chemistry, steam, and heat escalation require SDS/label controls, PPE, ventilation, flush/rinse controls, and documented fabric testing
testFirst: Test detergent, warmer water, enzyme detergent, oxidizer escalation, alcohol/solvent, acid spotter, or any pro chemistry on a hidden seam/hem with a clean white cloth before treating the visible area
safetyMatrix.colorfastnessTest: On a hidden seam or hem, press a clean white cloth with the planned detergent/water mix for one minute; stop if dye transfers, color changes, or texture changes.
safetyMatrix.flushNeutralizeDry: Rinse or flush out each cleaning agent before changing products; air-dry fully and inspect before any dryer, iron, heat, or professional escalation.
safetyMatrix.temperatureLimits: Use cool or care-label-safe lukewarm water on washable linen; no hot water, steam, dryer, or iron until residual oil is gone and the fabric has air-dried for inspection.
escalationTriggers: Stop and escalate on dye bleed, color loss, texture change, ring formation, spreading halo, or fabric distortion
Stop and escalate dry-clean-only, non-washable, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye items
Escalate spreading oil halo, ring formation, dye bleed, texture change, structured/valuable/vintage/dry-clean-only linen, or no improvement after one or two detergent attempts
stopAndEscalate: Stop and escalate on dye bleed, color loss, texture change, ring formation, spreading halo, or fabric distortion
Stop and escalate dry-clean-only, non-washable, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, high-value, or unstable-dye items
Escalate spreading oil halo, ring formation, dye bleed, texture change, structured/valuable/vintage/dry-clean-only linen, or no improvement after one or two detergent attempts
safetyMatrix.ppeVentilation: For Home detergent use, follow the product label and keep products away from children and pets. For concentrates or Pro chemistry, use gloves/eye protection as the SDS requires and work with ventilation.
safetyMatrix.homeVsProBoundary: Home guidance excludes professional-strength oxidizers, acids, solvents, named pro products, exact pro concentrations, steam, and heat escalation; those belong only in a professional workflow with SDS/label controls.

materialWarnings: Colored linen requires hidden hem/colorfastness testing
Residual oil plus heat can set a darker mark

escalation.when: Escalate spreading oil halo, ring formation, dye bleed, texture change, structured/valuable/vintage/dry-clean-only linen, unstable dye, or no improvement after one or two detergent attempts.
escalation.whatToTell: Olive oil on linen; item construction and care label; whether oil was blotted; detergent used; any spreading halo, ring, dye bleed, or texture change.
escalation.specialistType: professional textile cleaner or dry cleaner

contextQuestions: Is the linen washable, lined, structured, embellished, vintage, or high-value?
Is the stain fresh or aged?
Is the linen colored and did the hem test transfer dye?
Has heat or a dryer been used?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Avoid absolute water-first/oil claims; the supported Home move is blot/lift excess oil, then detergent on washable linen.

sources.title: University Stain Science (compiled extension reference)
sources.evidence: Consumer stain-family guidance for protein, tannin, oil, combination and unknown stains; blot rather than rub, work from the wrong side where appropriate, test first, and do not iron or dry until the stain is removed.

sources.title: EPA Alternative Cleaning Curriculum
sources.evidence: Safety and process gates: heat, concentration, oxidation, abrasion and mismanaged water can damage textiles; detergents/surfactants suspend oily soils; test hidden areas, follow product labels, ventilate, use PPE for concentrates, avoid incompatible mixes, and clean only according to care label or reject/escalate.

sources.title: GONR Material Safety Matrix v2
sources.evidence: Internal cotton/linen safety boundaries; protein, oil, tannin and oxidizer controls; dry-side solvent/pro chemistry boundaries; no hot water on protein stains; never mix chlorine bleach with ammonia or acids.

sources.title: R.R. Street 4Streets On-the-Spot
sources.evidence: Professional classification and operator workflow context for food/beverage, oil, tannin, protein and combination stains; pro-only product taxonomy.

sources.title: American Drycleaner Spotting Techniques
sources.evidence: Professional solvent/oil, tannin, flushing and controlled last-resort bleaching cautions; low suitability for Home copy.

sources.title: BÜFA DetaProfi Spotting Chart
sources.evidence: Professional chart support for oil, red wine, sauce/tannin and linen hem testing; rinse between products; pro-only chemistry sequence reference.
